sourceMovie.title: The Wave
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Wave (2015) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Roar Uthaug's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dante's Peak
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Wave clicked for you, Dante's Peak (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Wave?
faq.answer: Try Dante's Peak, The Towering Inferno, The Quake—each shares DNA with The Wave while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Wave?
faq.answer: The Wave (2015) is best described as Action, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Wave part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wave / Quake Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
